findings.title: EU-US tariff regime escalates with overlapping duties on steel, aluminum, and reciprocal measures
findings.summary: EU faces 25% US tariffs on steel and 10% on aluminum under Section 232, plus 2025-2026 reciprocal tariffs, prompting EU retaliation on US agriculture and machinery. This sustains high effective US tariff rates, pressuring EU export competitiveness and global trade flows. Impacts Metals and EM Equities via supply chain costs.
findings.claim_excerpt: EU steel and aluminum products face 25% and 10% tariffs respectively under Section 232... EU responded with retaliatory tariffs on American agricultural products, machinery.
findings.signal_type_basis: Ongoing enacted tariffs with retaliation represent medium-term trade disruption under active Tariff Shock Protocol.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Deepens US-EU economic coercion, eroding EU relative competitiveness vs. other exporters like China.
findings.confidence_basis: Multiple sources detail active overlapping tariff regimes and retaliation.

sources_cited.excerpt: EU steel and aluminum products face 25% and 10% tariffs... EU retaliatory tariffs on American agricultural products.
Turnberry deal locks in a 15% tariff rate for EU exports.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Two sources confirm enacted tariffs and retaliation from specified scope-equivalent sites.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Active EU retaliation and competitiveness erosion signal European fiscal autonomy pressures.
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Steel/aluminum tariffs heighten commodity input stress for EM manufacturers.

findings.review_reason: Assess if Turnberry deal ratification alters rung or adds digital services exposure.

findings.title: EU exports to US projected to slow 4.6% in 2026 from sustained high tariffs
findings.summary: Turnberry deal fixes 15% tariffs on EU exports, eroding EU edge vs. China/India as their rates converge lower. Shift from uncertainty to competitiveness drag intensifies trade response without frontrunning buffer. Affects Bonds via inflation passthrough and Real Estate construction costs.
findings.claim_excerpt: EU export growth to the US is expected to slow further by around 4.6% in 2026 due to tariffs alone.
findings.signal_type_basis: Protocol upgrades trade volume projections to CC under active tariff shock.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Tariff convergence signals persistent EU-US trade reallocation, not transient uncertainty.
findings.confidence_basis: Quantitative projection corroborated across analytical sources.

sources_cited.excerpt: EU export growth... slow further by around 4.6% in 2026 due to tariffs alone.
2025-2026 reciprocal tariff regime has introduced a new framework that further escalates duties.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Dual sources validate projection and deal context.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Deteriorating EU competitiveness vs. Asia accelerates fiscal divergence.

findings.review_reason: Track national ratification of Turnberry deal for rung upgrade potential.

findings.title: USMCA 2026 review heightens North American tariff and supply chain uncertainty
findings.summary: Ahead of July 1 review, US pushes stricter auto rules of origin and challenges Mexico energy policies, risking lapse of tariff protections for Canada/Mexico. Failure to renew could expose them to full US tariffs, stressing EM Equities and Energy sectors. Negotiations target Section 232 steel tariffs.
findings.claim_excerpt: Failure to renew the USMCA agreement would remove Canada and Mexico’s protections against U.S. tariffs.
findings.signal_type_basis: Pre-review positioning is episodic until July 1 enactment or breakdown.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Amplifies tariff shock for Mexico auto/energy exports if protections lapse.
findings.confidence_basis: Single detailed source; no secondary confirmation in window.

sources_cited.excerpt: USMCA review in 2026... failure to renew would remove Canada and Mexico’s protections against U.S. tariffs.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: One source only, pending broader coverage.

findings.episodic_reason: Pre-negotiation positioning ahead of July deadline.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Mexico energy policy disputes tie to regional AI capex supply chains.
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Rules of origin changes stress EM auto exports.

findings.review_reason: Monitor June 1 submissions for escalation signals.

findings.title: US launches new trade probe into EU as 7% of exports face >15% tariffs
findings.summary: European Commission rejects US probe as unjustified amid summer 2025 deal uncertainty, with 7% EU exports over 15% cap. Signals persistent dispute friction despite negotiations. Ties to Tech via potential digital services escalation.
findings.claim_excerpt: 7% of EU exports now face tariffs exceeding the 15% cap... US opens new probe.
findings.signal_type_basis: New probe is episodic probe absent sustained escalation.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Tests 2025 deal limits, risks digital tax retaliation.
findings.confidence_basis: Single source outside core 48h but relevant to active regime.

sources_cited.excerpt: Commission denies unfair trade practices as US opens new probe... 7% of EU exports face tariffs exceeding 15%.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Isolated report; requires follow-up for confirmation.

findings.episodic_reason: Single probe announcement without immediate retaliation.
findings.review_reason: Check for probe outcomes or WTO linkage.

below_threshold.title: EU-China de-risking via Germany Action Plan
below_threshold.reason_excluded: February 2026 dated; outside 48h window and not macro-financial move

tariff_escalation_status.rung_basis: Enacted Section 232/reciprocal tariffs with EU retaliation on US goods; Turnberry locks 15% rate.
